articles.title: Oxfmt Beta: A Fast, Rust-Powered JavaScript Code Formatter
articles.description: A 100% Prettier-compatible JavaScript code formatter (and sister project of Oxlint) that boasts being 30x faster than Prettier and 3x faster than Biome. Since the alpha, it now supports embedded language formatting (JSX, YAML, HTML, etc), Tailwind CSS class sorting, import sorting, and more.
articles.author: Boshen, Dunqing, and Sugiura (VoidZero)

articles.title: The Fastest Frontend Tooling for Humans and AI
articles.description: Christoph (of Jest fame) covers his preferred tools for getting your JavaScript tool stack running as fast as possible. It’s also intended for LLMs to process via this Markdown version.
articles.author: Christoph Nakazawa

articles.title: Goodbye innerHTML, Hello setHTML for Stronger XSS Protection
articles.description: As of v148, Firefox supports the Sanitizer API. Rather than assigning HTML to DOM nodes with innerHTML, you can use setHTML for safety by default. It’s a cutting-edge feature, with Chrome 146 (beta) also adding support, but nothing in Safari yet, so DOMPurify will remain useful for a while.
articles.author: Mozilla Hacks

articles.title: Halving Node.js Memory Usage with Pointer Compression
articles.description: Cloudflare, Igalia, and the Node project have collaborated on node-caged, a Node.js 25 Docker image with V8 pointer compression enabled, yielding up to 50% memory savings. Matteo digs into all the details.
articles.author: Matteo Collina

articles.title: OpenSeadragon 6.0: A Web Viewer for High Resolution Images
articles.description: A big step forward for a project that’s almost 15 years old, and one of few stable, trusty options for rendering ultra-high resolution images for users to zoom into and pan around. Version 6 introduces a new async and cache-managed pipeline, making it far more efficient at scale.
articles.author: OpenSeadragon Contributors

articles.title: bignumber.js 10.0: Library for Arbitrary-Precision Arithmetic
articles.description: Works around limitations of JavaScript’s Number and BigInt types, such as if you need to work with very large non-integers. Usefully, the library is included on the page so you can play with it in the JS console.
articles.author: Michael Mclaughlin

articles.title: ⏳ Slowmo: Slow Down, Pause, or Speed Up Time
articles.description: A tool you can use either as a library or browser extension that slows down time in the browser for debugging and testing purposes. It slows down numerous things including CSS animations, transitions, and requestAnimationFrame.
articles.author: Francois Laberge

articles.title: React Doctor: Give Your React Code a Quick Check-Up
articles.description: Fresh from the creator of React Scan and React Grab, a new tool that scans your codebase and gives you a 0-100 score. (GitHub repo.)
articles.author: Aiden Bai

articles.title: Vue Scrollama 3.0: Vue Component for Scroll-Driven Interactions
articles.description: Scrollama is a library for doing so called ‘scrollytelling’ where scroll position affects the presence of certain elements in the viewport. This project makes it easier to use with Vue.
articles.author: Vignesh Shenoy

articles.description: Martin Kleppe, known for his creative experiments like tixy.land, has created a scrolling bitmap quine in just 666 characters of HTML and JavaScript. Here's the annotated source so you can see how it works.
In the Oracle vs Deno JavaScript trademark case, Oracle has asked for a two month extension to proceedings, which Deno has agreed to.
Patrick Brosset notes that, as of last month's release of Firefox 147, you can now use JS modules in service workers on all major browsers.
A cute technical trick for using the browser's canvas element to compress textual data from JavaScript.
Vuetify 4.0 – Vue framework for building responsive UIs. (Upgrade docs.)
npm v11.10.0 – Maintainers can now add/update trusted publishing configs across multiple packages simultaneously. There's also a new --min-release-age option.
Node.js v25.7.0 (Current) and v24.14.0 (LTS)
ESLint 10.0.2, Hono 4.12, Deno 2.6.10, Electron 40.6, Three.js r183

articles.title: border-shape: The Future of the Non-Rectangular Web
articles.description: Una shares several fun experiments with the border-shape property, calling it a “game-changer” for geometry on the web platform. It’s an early-stage feature so support is still behind a Chromium flag right now.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: An In-Depth Guide to Customizing Lists with CSS
articles.description: Richard goes into plenty of detail here, with code examples aplenty. You’ll probably come away having learned something here.
articles.author: Richard Rutter

articles.title: Standard HTML Video & Audio Lazy-Loading is Coming
articles.description: A progress update from Scott who has been working on standardizing lazy-loading the HTML video and audio element. He explains that “the feature is not yet standard, but the HTML spec proposal, platform tests, and browser patches have been in review for many weeks and support just landed behind a flag in Chrome Canary”.
articles.author: Scott Jehl

articles.title: Sprites on the Web
articles.description: Josh shows us the basics of frame-based animation, doing away with GIFs in favor of a fast CSS approach. He shares how to use things like the object-fit property and the steps() timing function to create neat, small-scale animated sprites. As always, lots of good examples and demos.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: Everything You Never Wanted to Know About visually-hidden
articles.description: Visually hiding content always throws up some accessibility questions — this deep dive helps break it down.
articles.author: David Bushell

articles.title: ▶  Smart Layouts
articles.description: A roughly hour long talk in which Ahmad talks us through the process of using modern CSS to build layouts that are aware of their surroundings and context.
articles.author: Ahmad Shadeed

articles.title: Potentially Coming to a Browser :near() You
articles.description: Danny shares some ideas for how we could use the proposed :near() pseudo-class — a powerful suggested concept that would detect when a pointer is near an element.
articles.author: Daniel Schwarz

articles.title: Underlining Links with CSS
articles.description: Stuart shares his own reference guide for styling links beyond the norm. “CSS now gives us a whole set of text-decoration properties that let us get really specific about how our underlines should look and behave”.
articles.author: Stuart Robson

articles.title: Typographic Scales in CSS with :heading(), sibling-index(), and pow()
articles.description: Another from Stuart, this time looking at how to build flexible, maintainable typographic scales with just a few lines of code.
articles.author: Stuart Robson

articles.title: ▶  Interop 2026: Browsers Are Finally Catching Up
articles.description: Last week we shared news of the Interop efforts for this year, now Scott and Wes sit down to unpack the browser features looking to aligning across engines, from container style queries and anchor positioning to scroll-driven animations and view transitions.
articles.author: Syntax podcast

articles.title: The Four New Color Palettes in Tailwind CSS 4.2
articles.description: A quick look at the new color swatches (namely Mauve, Olive, Mist and Taupe) recently added to Tailwind CSS 4.2.
articles.author: Chris Brandrick

articles.title: Base.css: A Multi-Purpose, Classless Stylesheet with Semantic Rules
articles.description: Joining a very long line of similar solutions, Base.css claims to be the most semantic classless stylesheet, and it certainly makes a good pitch in this writeup, which doubles as a live demo.
articles.author: Toheeb Ogunbiyi

articles.title: OpenSeadragon 6.0: A Web Viewer for High Resolution Images
articles.description: One of few long-term, stable, and widely trusted options for rendering ultra-high resolution images for users to zoom into and pan around. Version 6 introduces a new async, cache-managed pipeline, making it far more efficient at scale.
articles.author: OpenSeadragon Contributors

articles.title: image-compare: Accessible Web Component for Comparing Images
articles.description: No dependency slider-style comparisons that work with keyboard input too. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Cloud Four

articles.title: PWAscore: Compare Progressive Web App Capabilities Across Popular Mobile Browsers
articles.description: Currently displays ‘scorecards’ for Chrome and Firefox on Android and Safari for iOS, based on 200+ PWA features and core features are weighted higher.
articles.author: Charles Wiltgen

articles.title: Introducing the Sugarcube Toolkit
articles.description: This frontend toolkit processes W3C DTCG design tokens into CSS variables, utility classes, and component styles. Works with Vite or as a standalone CLI. Docs here.
articles.author: Mark Tomlinson

articles.title: Page Gym: A Page Speed Test and Optimization Tool
articles.description: A compact tool that lets you choose from 3 regions, desktop or mobile, and will spit out data covering LCP, FCP, CLS, etc. One neat feature is ability to compare multiple perf tests, and you can log in to save your test history.
articles.author: Razvan Cojocariu
